summary: Saul's reign collapses in darkness — consulting a forbidden medium, abandoned by his Philistine allies, watching his army destroyed and his sons killed — while David pursues and defeats the Amalekites who burned Ziklag, establishing a just rule about shared plunder; Psalm 18 frames it all in David's long victory hymn celebrating God as his rock, fortress, and deliverer from every enemy including Saul.
psalm_rows_html:
PsalmTypeAuthorSituationKey ThemeNotable VerseNT Connection
18Royal Hymn of Thanksgiving / Victory SongDavidSuperscription: composed on the day the LORD delivered David from the hand of all his enemies and from the hand of Saul; also recorded in 2 Samuel 22God as rock, fortress, and deliverer; divine warrior theophany; reward for righteousness; universal royal dominionv.2 — "The LORD is my rock and my fortress and my deliverer, my God, my rock, in whom I take refuge, my shield, and the horn of my salvation, my stronghold."v.49 quoted by Paul in Romans 15:9 ("I will praise you among the Gentiles") as a Messianic text pointing to Christ's lordship over all nations; the psalm's portrait of the vindicated, victorious king is fulfilled in Christ's resurrection and exaltation
